tasks.title: Fix emitter to handle File body operations with raw binary instead of JSON serialization
tasks.description:
Fix the emitter so File body operations (Type.File.Body) use raw binary handling instead of JSON model serialization.

## Problem
The emitter generates fileSerializer/fileDeserializer functions that treat File objects as regular JSON models (with base64-encoded contents). For File body operations, this is incorrect:

### Uploads
- **Current**: Sends JSON body `{ contentType, filename, contents: base64 }` with binary Content-Type header
- **Expected**: Sends raw binary content (Uint8Array) as the body with the correct Content-Type header

### Downloads
- **Current**: Uses `fileDeserializer(result.body)` which accesses `result.body.contents`, but the response body IS the raw binary Buffer
- **Expected**: Should take the raw binary response body and wrap it in a File object: `{ contents: rawBuffer, contentType: responseContentType }`

## Affected scenarios (8 total, all in test/e2e/http/type/file/main.test.ts)
1. uploadFileSpecificContentType (image/png)
2. uploadFileJsonContentType (application/json)
3. uploadFileMultipleContentTypes (image/png | image/jpeg)
4. uploadFileDefaultContentType (any)
5. downloadFileSpecificContentType (image/png)
6. downloadFileJsonContentType (application/json)
7. downloadFileMultipleContentTypes (image/png | image/jpeg)
8. downloadFileDefaultContentType (any)

## What needs to change
The emitter needs to detect when an operation uses `@bodyRoot file: Http.File<...>` and:
- For uploads: Send `file.contents` as the raw body instead of `fileSerializer(file)`
- For downloads: Use getBinaryResponse or equivalent to read raw binary, then construct the File object

## E2E tests
Tests are already written and skipped in test/e2e/http/type/file/main.test.ts. Unskip them after fixing.

tasks.title: Fix multipart file/binary property types to match legacy output
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter generates simplified types for multipart file/binary properties (`Uint8Array`, `FileRequiredMetaData`) instead of the legacy's `FileContents` union pattern. This must be fixed to match legacy output for API surface parity.

**Legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce the same model interface types.**

## Problem — 12 affected interfaces in `payload/multipart` spec

The legacy emitter generates model interfaces where file/binary properties use the `FileContents | { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename?: string }` union pattern. The new emitter uses simpler types that break API parity.

### Specific differences

| Interface | Member | Legacy Type | New Type (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------|------------------|
| `MultiPartRequest` | `profileImage` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename?: string }` | `Uint8Array` |
| `ComplexHttpPartsModelRequest` | `profileImage` | `File \| { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename: string }` | `FileRequiredMetaData` |
| `BinaryArrayPartsRequest` | `pictures` | `Array<FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }>` | `Uint8Array[]` |
| `MultiBinaryPartsRequest` | `profileImage` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }` | `Uint8Array` |
| `MultiBinaryPartsRequest` | `picture` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }` | `Uint8Array` |
| `JsonPartRequest` | `profileImage` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }` | `Uint8Array` |
| `MultiPartOptionalRequest` | `profileImage` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }` | `Uint8Array` |
| `MultiPartRequestWithWireName` | `image` | `FileContents \| { contents: FileContents; ... }` | `Uint8Array` |

Additionally, the legacy defines and exports the `FileContents` type:
```typescript
export type FileContents = string | NodeJS.ReadableStream | ReadableStream<Uint8Array> | Uint8Array | Blob;
```

And some interfaces have `contentType`/`filename` convenience members that are absent in the new output.

### For HTTP parts scenarios, the legacy uses inline object types:
- `File | { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename: string }` (required filename)
- `FileContents | { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename?: string }` (optional filename)

The new emitter uses named types (`FileRequiredMetaData`, `FileSpecificContentType`) which differ from legacy.

## Root Cause

In `src/components/type-expression.tsx` (line ~128-130), ALL `bytes` SdkType kinds are mapped to `Uint8Array`:
```typescript
case "bytes":
  return "Uint8Array";
```

There is no multipart-specific type generation. The multipart serializer (`src/components/serialization/multipart-serializer.tsx`) handles the runtime conversion via `createFilePartDescriptor()` which accepts the broader `FileContents` union, but the **model interface types** exposed to consumers don't reflect this flexibility.

## What to Fix

### 1. Model interface types for multipart file properties
When a model property is used in a multipart context and represents a file/binary part, the type in the model interface should be the `FileContents | { contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename?: string }` union pattern (matching legacy), NOT bare `Uint8Array`.

The TCGC metadata `multipart?.isFilePart` flag on `SdkBodyModelPropertyType` indicates which properties are file parts. Use this to determine the correct type.

### 2. FileContents type export
The `FileContents` type must be exported. It's currently defined in `src/components/static-helpers/multipart-helpers.tsx` as a runtime helper type. Ensure it appears in the public API.

### 3. HTTP parts typed interfaces
For HTTP parts scenarios (where TCGC metadata specifies required contentType or filename), the inline object type `{ contents: FileContents; contentType?: string; filename: string }` should be used instead of named types like `FileRequiredMetaData`.

## Key Files to Modify

- `src/components/type-expression.tsx` — Add multipart-aware type expression for bytes properties
- `src/components/model-interface.tsx` — Detect multipart file properties and apply correct type (see `getPropertyTypeExpression` function around line 458)
- `src/components/static-helpers/multipart-helpers.tsx` — Ensure `FileContents` type is exported
- `src/components/serialization/multipart-serializer.tsx` — May need adjustment if model types change

## Reference Files

- Legacy output: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/payload/multipart/src/index.d.ts`
- New emitter output: `test/e2e/generated/payload/multipart/src/models/models.ts`
- E2E tests: `test/e2e/http/payload/multipart/main.test.ts` (16 tests, all currently passing — must remain passing after fix)

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Regenerate payload/multipart output and compare model interface types with legacy
4. All 16 existing multipart e2e tests must continue to pass

tasks.title: Fix discriminated union member types to use union aliases instead of base types (Category 6a)
tasks.description:
## Summary
Model properties typed as a discriminated base type use the base type name (`Fish`, `Bird`) instead of the union alias (`FishUnion`, `BirdUnion`). **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce the same member types.**

## Problem

When a model property references a polymorphic base type that has a discriminated union alias, the new emitter uses the base type directly instead of the union alias. This loses type narrowing ability for consumers.

### Affected specs and exact differences

**`type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator` spec:**

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `FishUnion` (type alias) | — | `SharkUnion \| Salmon \| Fish` | `Shark \| Salmon \| Fish` |
| `Salmon` | `partner` | `FishUnion` | `Fish` |
| `Salmon` | `friends` | `FishUnion[]` | `Fish[]` |
| `Salmon` | `hate` | `Record<string, FishUnion>` | `Record<string, Fish>` |

**`type/model/inheritance/single-discriminator` spec:**

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `Eagle` | `partner` | `BirdUnion` | `Bird` |
| `Eagle` | `friends` | `BirdUnion[]` | `Bird[]` |
| `Eagle` | `hate` | `Record<string, BirdUnion>` | `Record<string, Bird>` |

### FishUnion type alias issue

Additionally, `FishUnion` itself should contain `SharkUnion` (not `Shark`):
- Legacy: `type FishUnion = SharkUnion | Salmon | Fish`
- New: `type FishUnion = Shark | Salmon | Fish`

The `SharkUnion` type includes `SawShark | GoblinShark | Shark`, preserving the full nested discrimination hierarchy. Using `Shark` directly loses the ability to narrow to `SawShark` or `GoblinShark`.

## Root Cause

Two separate issues:

### Issue 1: Member type resolution uses base type instead of union alias

In `src/components/type-expression.tsx`, when a property type is an `SdkModelType` that has a polymorphic union alias (`FishUnion`, `BirdUnion`), the emitter resolves to the base model's refkey (`Fish`, `Bird`) instead of the union alias's refkey.

The fix needs to detect when an `SdkModelType` is a discriminated base (has `discriminatedSubtypes`) and use the `polymorphicTypeRefkey(type)` (pointing to the `*Union` alias) instead of `typeRefkey(type)` (pointing to the interface).

Key function: `getTypeExpression()` in `src/components/type-expression.tsx` around line 100 (case "model").
Key refkey: `polymorphicTypeRefkey()` in `src/utils/refkeys.ts` — already exists and is used by `PolymorphicType` component.

### Issue 2: PolymorphicType uses base type instead of union alias for subtypes

In `src/components/polymorphic-type.tsx`, the `PolymorphicType` component generates the union type alias. When enumerating subtypes, it uses `typeRefkey(subtype)` which resolves to the model interface (e.g., `Shark`). For subtypes that themselves have discriminated subtypes (e.g., `Shark` has `SawShark`/`GoblinShark`), it should use `polymorphicTypeRefkey(subtype)` (resolving to `SharkUnion`) instead.

Key function: `PolymorphicType` in `src/components/polymorphic-type.tsx` around line 47 — the `<For>` loop rendering subtypes.

## What to Fix

### Fix 1: `src/components/type-expression.tsx`
In `getTypeExpression()`, when handling `case "model"`:
- Check if the model has `discriminatedSubtypes` (is a polymorphic base)
- If yes, return `polymorphicTypeRefkey(type)` instead of `typeRefkey(type)`
- This makes `Salmon.partner` resolve to `FishUnion` instead of `Fish`

### Fix 2: `src/components/polymorphic-type.tsx`
In the `<For>` loop rendering subtypes:
- For each subtype, check if it itself has `discriminatedSubtypes` (a nested base)
- If yes, use `polymorphicTypeRefkey(subtype)` instead of `typeRefkey(subtype)`
- This makes `FishUnion = SharkUnion | Salmon | Fish` instead of `FishUnion = Shark | Salmon | Fish`

## Key Files

- `src/components/type-expression.tsx` — `getTypeExpression()` function, model case
- `src/components/polymorphic-type.tsx` — `PolymorphicType` component, subtype rendering
- `src/utils/refkeys.ts` — `polymorphicTypeRefkey()` and `typeRefkey()` definitions

## Reference Files

- Legacy nested-discriminator: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator/src/index.d.ts`
- Legacy single-discriminator: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/model/inheritance/single-discriminator/src/index.d.ts`
- New emitter nested-discriminator: `test/e2e/generated/type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator/src/models/models.ts`
- New emitter single-discriminator: `test/e2e/generated/type/model/inheritance/single-discriminator/src/models/models.ts`

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Verify `Salmon.partner` is `FishUnion` (not `Fish`), `Eagle.partner` is `BirdUnion` (not `Bird`)
4. Verify `FishUnion = SharkUnion | Salmon | Fish` (not `Shark | Salmon | Fish`)
5. Verify serializers still work correctly — `salmonSerializer` should call `fishUnionSerializer` for partner

tasks.title: Fix extensible enum generation to match legacy Known{Name} + string pattern (Category 6b)
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter generates extensible enums as string literal unions (e.g., `type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = "b" | "c"`) instead of the legacy's `Known{Name}` enum + string type alias pattern. **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce the same enum pattern.**

## Background: `experimental-extensible-enums` Option

Both the legacy and new emitters have an `experimental-extensible-enums` boolean option that controls how extensible enums (TCGC `SdkEnumType` with `isFixed: false`) are rendered.

### When `experimental-extensible-enums: true`:
```typescript
// Type alias = broad string (accepts unknown values)
export type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = string;

// Known enum with all predefined values
export enum KnownStringExtensibleNamedUnion {
  OptionB = "b",
  C = "c",
}
```

### When `experimental-extensible-enums: false` (default):
```typescript
// Legacy: string literal union WITH string fallback
export type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = "b" | "c" | string;

// New emitter (WRONG): string literal union WITHOUT string fallback
export type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = "b" | "c";
```

**Neither pattern generates a `Known{Name}` enum when the flag is false.**

## Problem

The legacy `type/union` spec was generated with `experimental-extensible-enums: true` (confirmed in legacy tspconfig at `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/tspconfig.yaml`). The new emitter's `type/union` e2e test does NOT set this option, so it defaults to `false`, producing a different output.

### Exact differences in `type/union` spec

| Type | Legacy (with flag=true) | New (with flag=false) |
|------|-------------------------|----------------------|
| `StringExtensibleNamedUnion` | `string` | `"b" \| "c"` |
| `KnownStringExtensibleNamedUnion` | `enum { OptionB = "b", C = "c" }` | _(missing)_ |

### Two issues to fix

**Issue 1**: The `type/union` e2e test needs `experimental-extensible-enums: true` in its tspconfig to match the legacy configuration.

**Issue 2**: When `experimental-extensible-enums: false` (default), the new emitter generates `"b" | "c"` but the legacy generates `"b" | "c" | string`. The new emitter is missing the `| string` fallback for extensible enums. This matters for specs that DON'T set the flag — extensible enums should still accept unknown string values at runtime.

## How `experimental-extensible-enums` Works in the New Emitter

The option is already implemented:
- **Definition**: `src/lib.ts` lines 46-51 — boolean option in emitter schema
- **Context**: `src/context/emitter-options-context.tsx` line 40-49 — `experimentalExtensibleEnums` field
- **Wiring**: `src/emitter.tsx` line 260 — reads from `context.options`
- **Enum generation**: `src/components/enum-declaration.tsx` line 67:
  ```typescript
  const shouldEmitKnownEnum = !type.isFixed && experimentalExtensibleEnums;
  ```
  - When `true`: generates `type Name = string` + `enum KnownName { ... }` ✅ (matches legacy)
  - When `false`: generates `type Name = "val1" | "val2"` ❌ (missing `| string` fallback)

## What to Fix

### Fix 1: Add `| string` fallback for extensible enums when flag is false
In `src/components/enum-declaration.tsx`, when rendering the type alias body for extensible enums (`isFixed: false`) with `experimentalExtensibleEnums: false`, append `| string` to the literal union:
- Current: `"b" | "c"`
- Expected: `"b" | "c" | string`

Look at function around line 205 (`getTypeAliasBody` or similar) that generates the union members. For extensible enums (not fixed), after listing all literal values, add `| string` (or `| number` for numeric enums) as a fallback.

### Fix 2: Configure `type/union` e2e test with `experimental-extensible-enums: true`
Create or update `test/e2e/http/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` to include:
```yaml
options:
  http-client-js:
    experimental-extensible-enums: true
```
This matches the legacy test configuration and will produce `Known{Name}` enum + string type alias pattern.

## Key Files

- `src/components/enum-declaration.tsx` — Enum/type alias generation logic
- `src/lib.ts` — Option schema definition
- `src/context/emitter-options-context.tsx` — Option context
- `test/e2e/http/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` — E2E test config (create if missing)

## Reference Files

- Legacy tspconfig: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` (has `experimental-extensible-enums: true`)
- Legacy output: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/src/index.d.ts`
- New emitter output: `test/e2e/generated/type/union/src/models/models.ts`
- New emitter scenario tests for enums: `test/scenarios/cases/enumUnion/enumUnion.md` (uses `experimental-extensible-enums: true`)

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. With `experimental-extensible-enums: true`: verify `type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = string` + `enum KnownStringExtensibleNamedUnion { ... }`
4. With `experimental-extensible-enums: false`: verify `type StringExtensibleNamedUnion = "b" | "c" | string` (note the `| string` fallback)
5. Fixed enums (`isFixed: true`) should NOT be affected — they remain exact literal unions without `| string`

tasks.title: Fix discriminator property type narrowing on intermediate base models (Category 6c)
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter incorrectly narrows discriminator properties on intermediate base models. `Shark.sharktype` is rendered as `"shark"` (literal) instead of `string` (broad type). **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce the same discriminator property types.**

## Problem

In the `type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator` spec, the `Shark` model is BOTH:
1. A **subtype** of `Fish` (with `kind: "shark"` — its discriminator value in Fish's hierarchy)
2. A **base type** for its own subtypes (`SawShark`, `GoblinShark`) discriminated by `sharktype`

The new emitter incorrectly applies the discriminator literal narrowing to `Shark.sharktype`:

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `Shark` | `sharktype` | `string` | `"shark"` |
| `Shark` | `kind` | `"shark"` | `"shark"` |

`Shark.kind: "shark"` is correct — it's the discriminator value for the Fish hierarchy.
`Shark.sharktype: "shark"` is WRONG — this is Shark's OWN discriminator, and as a base type it should be `string` (or a union of its subtypes' values), not narrowed to a literal.

## Root Cause

In `src/components/model-interface.tsx`, the `getPropertyTypeExpression` function (lines ~458-474):

```typescript
function getPropertyTypeExpression(
  property: SdkModelPropertyType,
  model: SdkModelType,
): Children {
  if (property.discriminator && model.discriminatorValue) {
    return `"${model.discriminatorValue}"`;
  }
  // ...
}
```

The bug: This check treats ALL discriminator properties the same. When rendering `Shark`:
- `property.discriminator` is true for `sharktype` (it IS a discriminator)
- `model.discriminatorValue` is `"shark"` (Shark's value for the PARENT `Fish.kind` discriminator)
- Result: `"shark"` is incorrectly applied to `sharktype`

The code conflates two different discriminator levels:
- `Shark.kind` should get the literal `"shark"` (Shark's discriminator value in Fish hierarchy)
- `Shark.sharktype` should get `string` (Shark is the base type for this discriminator)

## What to Fix

In `src/components/model-interface.tsx`, modify `getPropertyTypeExpression` to distinguish between:

1. **The parent's discriminator property on this model** — should use the literal value. This is the property that matches the parent hierarchy's discriminator (e.g., `kind` on `Shark` when `Shark extends Fish`).

2. **This model's own discriminator property for its subtypes** — should use the broad type (`string`). This is the property that this model uses to discriminate between ITS OWN subtypes (e.g., `sharktype` on `Shark` when `Shark` has `SawShark`/`GoblinShark`).

### Approach

The fix needs to check whether `property` is the model's OWN `discriminatorProperty` (meaning this model is a base for further discrimination). TCGC provides:
- `model.discriminatorProperty` — the property THIS model uses to discriminate its own subtypes
- `model.discriminatorValue` — the value THIS model uses for its parent's discriminator
- `property.discriminator` — whether this property is a discriminator (at any level)

Updated logic:
```typescript
function getPropertyTypeExpression(property, model) {
  if (property.discriminator && model.discriminatorValue) {
    // Don't narrow if this is the model's OWN discriminator property
    // (the model is a base type for this discriminator)
    if (model.discriminatorProperty && property === model.discriminatorProperty) {
      // This model's own discriminator — use broad type
      return getOptionalAwareTypeExpression(property.type, ...);
    }
    // Parent's discriminator — use literal value
    return `"${model.discriminatorValue}"`;
  }
  return getOptionalAwareTypeExpression(property.type, ...);
}
```

Note: Property identity comparison (`property === model.discriminatorProperty`) may need to use name comparison or TCGC identity. Verify how TCGC represents this — it may be `model.discriminatorProperty.name === property.name` or object identity.

## Key Files

- `src/components/model-interface.tsx` — `getPropertyTypeExpression` function (~line 458)

## Reference Files

- Legacy nested-discriminator: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator/src/index.d.ts`
  - `Shark.kind: "shark"` ✅ (narrowed — parent discriminator)
  - `Shark.sharktype: string` ✅ (broad — own discriminator base)
- New emitter output: `test/e2e/generated/type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator/src/models/models.ts`
  - `Shark.kind: "shark"` ✅
  - `Shark.sharktype: "shark"` ❌ (should be `string`)

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Verify `Shark.sharktype` is `string` (not `"shark"`)
4. Verify `Shark.kind` is still `"shark"` (parent discriminator still narrowed)
5. Verify `SawShark.sharktype` is `"saw"` (leaf subtypes still narrowed)
6. Verify `GoblinShark.sharktype` is `"goblin"` (leaf subtypes still narrowed)
7. Verify `Fish.kind` is `string` (root base type still broad)

tasks.title: Fix additionalProperties union types to use inline unions instead of named type aliases (Category 8a)
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter generates named type aliases for union types used in `additionalProperties` (e.g., `_MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty = string | number`) and references those aliases in the interface member type. **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce inline union types, not named type aliases, for these property types.**

## Problem

When a model with `additionalProperties` has a union value type, the new emitter creates an intermediate named union alias with a `_` prefix and references it from the `additionalProperties` member. The legacy emitter inlines the union members directly.

### Exact differences in `type/property/additional-properties` spec

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `MultipleSpreadRecord` | `additionalProperties` | `Record<string, string | number>` | `Record<string, _MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty>` |
| `SpreadRecordForUnion` | `additionalProperties` | `Record<string, string | number>` | `Record<string, _SpreadRecordForUnionAdditionalProperty>` |
| `SpreadRecordForNonDiscriminatedUnion` | `additionalProperties` | `Record<string, WidgetData0 | WidgetData1>` | `Record<string, _SpreadRecordForNonDiscriminatedUnionAdditionalProperty>` |

The named aliases DO resolve to the correct union:
```typescript
export type _MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty = string | number;
export type _SpreadRecordForNonDiscriminatedUnionAdditionalProperty = WidgetData0 | WidgetData1;
```

But the legacy never creates these aliases — it inlines the union directly in the `Record<string, ...>` value type.

## Root Cause

In `src/components/type-expression.tsx`, the `case "union"` handler always returns `typeRefkey(type)`:

```typescript
case "union":
  return typeRefkey(type);
```

This creates a named type reference for ALL union types, including auto-generated ones. For `SdkUnionType` values with `isGeneratedName === true` (e.g., `_MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty`), the correct behavior is to inline the union members rather than referencing a named alias.

The `additionalProperties` member is built in `src/components/model-interface.tsx` in `buildExplicitAdditionalPropertiesMember()`, which calls `getTypeExpression(apType)`. When `apType` is a generated union (`isGeneratedName === true`), this produces a refkey reference instead of an inline expression.

## What to Fix

### Fix 1: Inline generated unions in `type-expression.tsx`

In `getTypeExpression()`, modify the `case "union"` handler to check `type.isGeneratedName`:

```typescript
case "union":
  if (type.isGeneratedName) {
    // Inline the union members instead of referencing a named alias.
    // Must use Alloy code template to handle refkey variants (e.g., model types).
    // See how <For> with joiner=" | " is used in union-declaration.tsx for reference.
  }
  return typeRefkey(type);
```

The inline expression must use Alloy's `code` template to properly handle refkey variants (e.g., `WidgetData0 | WidgetData1` where model types need refkeys for cross-file imports). A simple string join won't work for non-primitive variants. Refer to `src/components/union-declaration.tsx` which already renders union variants correctly using `<For each={type.variantTypes} joiner=" | ">`.

### Fix 2: Stop emitting named type declarations for generated unions in `model-files.tsx`

The `namedUnions` array in `ModelsFile` currently includes ALL union types. Generated unions (`isGeneratedName === true`) should not be emitted as `UnionDeclaration` since they will be inlined everywhere:

```typescript
const namedUnions = unions
  .map((u) => (u.kind === "nullable" && u.type.kind === "union" ? u.type : u))
  .filter((u): u is SdkUnionType => u.kind === "union" && !u.isGeneratedName);
```

**IMPORTANT:** Before removing the declaration, verify that no serializer or deserializer references the generated union type by name. See Fix 3 below.

### Fix 3: Remove generated union helper functions from serializers/deserializers

Model deserializers currently call generated union helper functions. For example:

```typescript
// Current (wrong) model deserializer
export function multipleSpreadRecordDeserializer(item: any): MultipleSpreadRecord {
  return {
    additionalProperties: deserializeRecord(item, (v: any) => _multipleSpreadRecordAdditionalPropertyDeserializer(v), ["flag"]),
    ...
  };
}
// Current (wrong) generated helper — references the named alias
export function _multipleSpreadRecordAdditionalPropertyDeserializer(item: any): _MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty {
  return item;
}
```

When the named type alias is removed:
1. The `outputUnions` filter in `model-files.tsx` must also exclude generated unions:
   ```typescript
   const outputUnions = namedUnions.filter(
     (u) => !u.isGeneratedName && ((u.usage & UsageFlags.Output) !== 0 || ...)
   );
   ```
2. The `typeHasDeserializerDeclaration` predicate in `src/utils/serialization-predicates.ts` must return `false` for generated unions (check existing logic and add `!type.isGeneratedName` if not already present).
3. In the model deserializer, `_multipleSpreadRecordAdditionalPropertyDeserializer` is called because `needsTransformation(apType)` returns true for unions with Output usage. After the fix, `needsTransformation` should return `false` for generated unions, so the deserializer calls them directly without the helper. Review `src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx` `needsTransformation()` case `"union"` (around line 453).

The legacy emitter does NOT have `_multipleSpreadRecordAdditionalPropertyDeserializer` — it handles `additionalProperties` directly without generated union helper functions.

## Key Files

- `src/components/type-expression.tsx` — `getTypeExpression()`, `case "union"` (~line 184)
- `src/components/model-interface.tsx` — `buildExplicitAdditionalPropertiesMember()` (~line 210)
- `src/components/model-files.tsx` — `namedUnions` filter (~line 111), `outputUnions` filter (~line 201)
- `src/utils/serialization-predicates.ts` — `typeHasSerializerDeclaration`, `typeHasDeserializerDeclaration`
- `src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx` — `needsTransformation()` union case (~line 453)

## Reference Files

- **Legacy output** (correct): `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/property/additional-properties/src/index.d.ts`
  - `MultipleSpreadRecord.additionalProperties?: Record<string, string | number>` ✅
  - `SpreadRecordForUnion.additionalProperties?: Record<string, string | number>` ✅
  - `SpreadRecordForNonDiscriminatedUnion.additionalProperties?: Record<string, WidgetData0 | WidgetData1>` ✅
  - No `_MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty` type alias anywhere ✅
- **New emitter output** (wrong): `test/e2e/generated/type/property/additional-properties/src/models/models.ts`
  - Search for `_MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty`, `_SpreadRecordForUnionAdditionalProperty`, etc.

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Verify `MultipleSpreadRecord.additionalProperties` is `Record<string, string | number>` (not `Record<string, _MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty>`)
4. Verify `SpreadRecordForNonDiscriminatedUnion.additionalProperties` is `Record<string, WidgetData0 | WidgetData1>`
5. Verify no `_MultipleSpreadRecordAdditionalProperty`, `_SpreadRecordForUnionAdditionalProperty`, etc. type aliases exist
6. Verify no `_multipleSpreadRecordAdditionalPropertyDeserializer` function exists
7. Verify no `<Unresolved Symbol>` strings appear in any generated file

tasks.title: Fix literal union property types to use inline unions instead of named type aliases (Category 8b)
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter generates named type aliases for literal union types used as model property types (e.g., `UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty = "hello" | "world"`) and references those aliases in the interface. **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce inline union types directly in the interface member, not named type aliases.**

## Problem

When a model property has a union of literals (e.g., `"hello" | "world"`, `42 | 43`, `43.125 | 46.875`), the new emitter creates an intermediate named union alias and references it. The legacy emitter inlines the literals directly in the property type.

### Exact differences in `type/property/value-types` spec

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `UnionStringLiteralProperty` | `property` | `"hello" | "world"` | `UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty` |
| `UnionIntLiteralProperty` | `property` | `42 | 43` | `UnionIntLiteralPropertyProperty` |
| `UnionFloatLiteralProperty` | `property` | `43.125 | 46.875` | `UnionFloatLiteralPropertyProperty` |

### Exact differences in `type/property/optionality` spec

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `UnionStringLiteralProperty` | `property` | `"hello" | "world"` | `UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty` |
| `UnionIntLiteralProperty` | `property` | `1 | 2` | `UnionIntLiteralPropertyProperty` |
| `UnionFloatLiteralProperty` | `property` | `1.25 | 2.375` | `UnionFloatLiteralPropertyProperty` |

The named aliases DO resolve to the correct types:
```typescript
export type UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty = "hello" | "world";
export type UnionIntLiteralPropertyProperty = 42 | 43;
```
But the legacy never creates these aliases — it inlines directly.

## Root Cause

Same root cause as Category 8a (task `E2E-FIX-UNION-ADDITIONAL-PROPS-INLINE`).

In `src/components/type-expression.tsx`, `case "union"` always returns `typeRefkey(type)` for ALL union types, including auto-generated ones (`isGeneratedName === true`). Property types flow through `getPropertyTypeExpression()` -> `getOptionalAwareTypeExpression()` -> `getTypeExpression()` -> returns a named refkey instead of inline.

## What to Fix

This task depends on `E2E-FIX-UNION-ADDITIONAL-PROPS-INLINE`. The fix applied there (inlining `isGeneratedName === true` unions in `type-expression.tsx` and stopping their emission in `model-files.tsx`) should resolve these 8b cases as well.

After `E2E-FIX-UNION-ADDITIONAL-PROPS-INLINE` is merged, regenerate the affected specs and verify that 8b is resolved. If not, investigate whether `UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty` etc. have `isGeneratedName === true` or not.

### Serializer/Deserializer Impact

Current output includes pass-through helpers:
```typescript
// Wrong — should not exist
export function unionStringLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer(item: UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty): any {
  return item;
}
// Wrong — model serializer calling the above helper
export function unionStringLiteralPropertySerializer(item: UnionStringLiteralProperty): any {
  return { property: unionStringLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer(item["property"]) };
}
```

After the fix, the model serializer should inline directly:
```typescript
// Correct
export function unionStringLiteralPropertySerializer(item: UnionStringLiteralProperty): any {
  return { property: item["property"] };
}
```

The pass-through helpers `unionStringLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer`, `unionIntLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer`, `unionFloatLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer` should be removed. The legacy does NOT have these.

The same applies to deserializer side if similar pass-through deserializers exist.

## Key Files

- `src/components/type-expression.tsx` — `getTypeExpression()`, `case "union"` (~line 184)
- `src/components/model-interface.tsx` — `getPropertyTypeExpression()` (~line 458)
- `src/components/model-files.tsx` — `namedUnions` filter (~line 111), `outputUnions` filter (~line 201)
- `src/utils/serialization-predicates.ts` — serializer/deserializer predicates
- `src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx` — model serializer, `needsTransformation()` union case

## Reference Files

- **Legacy output** (correct):
  - `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/property/value-types/src/index.d.ts`
    - `UnionStringLiteralProperty.property: "hello" | "world"` ✅ (no named alias)
  - `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/property/optionality/src/index.d.ts`
    - `UnionStringLiteralProperty.property?: "hello" | "world"` ✅ (no named alias)
- **New emitter output** (wrong):
  - `test/e2e/generated/type/property/value-types/src/models/models.ts`
  - `test/e2e/generated/type/property/optionality/src/models/models.ts`

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Verify `UnionStringLiteralProperty.property` is `"hello" | "world"` (not a named alias)
4. Verify `UnionIntLiteralProperty.property` is `42 | 43` (not a named alias)
5. Verify `UnionFloatLiteralProperty.property` is `43.125 | 46.875` (not a named alias)
6. Verify the same for the `optionality` spec
7. Verify no `UnionStringLiteralPropertyProperty`, `UnionIntLiteralPropertyProperty`, `UnionFloatLiteralPropertyProperty` type aliases exist
8. Verify no pass-through helpers (`unionStringLiteralPropertyPropertySerializer`, etc.) exist
9. Verify no `<Unresolved Symbol>` strings in any generated file

tasks.title: Fix complex union member types to use inline unions instead of named type aliases (Category 8c)
tasks.description:
## Summary
The new emitter generates named type aliases for complex union property types in the `type/union` spec (e.g., `EnumsOnlyCasesLr`, `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral`) and references them from interface members. **The legacy output is the source of truth. The new emitter must produce inline union types directly in the interface members, not named type aliases.**

## Problem

When interface members have complex union types (enum combinations, mixed literals, mixed model+literal unions), the new emitter creates named aliases and references them. The legacy emitter inlines the union members directly.

### Exact differences in `type/union` spec

| Interface | Member | Legacy (correct) | New (wrong) |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-------------|
| `EnumsOnlyCases` | `lr` | `"left" | "right" | "up" | "down"` | `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` |
| `MixedLiteralsCases` | `stringLiteral` | `"a" | 2 | 3.3 | true` | `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral` |
| `MixedTypesCases` | `model` | `Cat | "a" | number | boolean` | `_MixedTypesCasesModel` |
| `StringAndArrayCases` | `string` | `string | string[]` | `_StringAndArrayCasesString` |

The new emitter output also generates intermediate types `LR = "left" | "right"` and `UD = "up" | "down"` (from TypeSpec `enum LR { left, right }` and `enum UD { up, down }`), which the legacy does NOT export at all.

**Legacy output for `EnumsOnlyCases`:**
```typescript
export declare interface EnumsOnlyCases {
  lr: "left" | "right" | "up" | "down";
  ud: "up" | "down";
}
```
No `LR`, `UD`, or `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` type aliases exist in the legacy.

## TypeSpec Source Context

The `type/union` TypeSpec spec defines:
```typespec
enum LR { left, right }
enum UD { up, down }

model EnumsOnlyCases {
  lr: LR | UD;   // union of two enums
  ud: UD | UD;
}
alias MixedLiteralsUnion = "a" | 2 | 3.3 | true;
model MixedLiteralsCases {
  stringLiteral: MixedLiteralsUnion;
  // ...
}
alias MixedTypesUnion = Cat | "a" | int32 | boolean;
model MixedTypesCases {
  model: MixedTypesUnion;
  // ...
}
model StringAndArrayCases {
  string: string | string[];
  // ...
}
```

The legacy tspconfig for `type/union` uses `experimental-extensible-enums: true`.
Reference: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/tspconfig.yaml`

## Root Cause and Complexity

This category has TWO distinct sub-issues:

### Sub-issue 1: Generated-name unions (`_` prefix, `isGeneratedName === true`)

Types: `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral`, `_MixedTypesCasesModel`, `_StringAndArrayCasesString`, `_StringAndArrayCasesArray`

These have `isGeneratedName === true` and will be resolved by the fix in `E2E-FIX-UNION-ADDITIONAL-PROPS-INLINE` (inlining generated unions in `type-expression.tsx`). After that task is merged, verify these are resolved.

### Sub-issue 2: `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` — wraps TypeSpec enums, `isGeneratedName === false`

`EnumsOnlyCasesLr = LR | UD` has a serializer (`enumsOnlyCasesLrSerializer`), which means `isGeneratedName === false` (the `inputUnions` filter in `model-files.tsx` excludes generated unions from serializer generation). The `isGeneratedName` fix won't automatically resolve this case.

**This sub-issue is dependent on `E2E-FIX-EXTENSIBLE-ENUM-KNOWN-PATTERN`** which adds `experimental-extensible-enums: true` to `test/e2e/http/type/union/tspconfig.yaml`. That flag change affects how TypeSpec enums `LR` and `UD` are rendered.

After `E2E-FIX-EXTENSIBLE-ENUM-KNOWN-PATTERN` is applied, re-run the `type/union` generation and check:
- Does `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` still appear?
- Are `LR` and `UD` still generated as separate type aliases?

If `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` still appears after the flag change, the agent must investigate deeper:

1. **Check `EnumsOnlyCasesLr.isGeneratedName` value** — run a debug TypeSpec compilation with TCGC and inspect the union type. Add a temporary `console.error(JSON.stringify(type, null, 2))` in `type-expression.tsx` for `case "union"` to observe TCGC's data.
2. **If `isGeneratedName === true`** — the fix from 8a should have already covered this. Re-check the filter.
3. **If `isGeneratedName === false` with the flag** — investigate why TCGC considers this a user-named type and whether the emitter should treat unions of enum types differently (e.g., expand enum values to their string literals instead of creating a named alias).

## What to Fix

### Step 1: Verify sub-issue 1 is resolved by task E2E-FIX-UNION-ADDITIONAL-PROPS-INLINE

After that task is done, `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral`, `_MixedTypesCasesModel`, `_StringAndArrayCasesString` should be inlined. If not, apply the same fix manually for these cases.

### Step 2: Apply `experimental-extensible-enums: true` (from task E2E-FIX-EXTENSIBLE-ENUM-KNOWN-PATTERN)

Ensure `test/e2e/http/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` has:
```yaml
options:
  http-client-js:
    experimental-extensible-enums: true
```

Re-run generation and verify `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` behavior.

### Step 3: Fix `EnumsOnlyCasesLr` if still present

If `EnumsOnlyCasesLr = LR | UD` still appears (with `isGeneratedName === false`), the fix approach is:

**Option A**: In `type-expression.tsx`, add logic to detect when a union type's variants are all enum types, and inline by expanding their enum values to string/numeric literals. For `LR | UD` this would produce `"left" | "right" | "up" | "down"`. Use `type.variantTypes` and check if each variant is `kind === "enum"` or `kind === "enumvalue"`, then map to their literal values.

**Option B**: Investigate if `EnumsOnlyCasesLr.isGeneratedName` is actually `true` and the isGeneratedName fix just needs to be ensured to cover this case.

### Step 4: Remove generated union serializer/deserializer helpers

After inlining:
- Remove `enumsOnlyCasesLrSerializer` and `enumsOnlyCasesUdSerializer` helper functions
- Update `enumsOnlyCasesSerializer` to NOT call these helpers:
  ```typescript
  // Before (wrong)
  export function enumsOnlyCasesSerializer(item: EnumsOnlyCases): any {
    return { lr: enumsOnlyCasesLrSerializer(item["lr"]), ... };
  }
  // After (correct)
  export function enumsOnlyCasesSerializer(item: EnumsOnlyCases): any {
    return { lr: item["lr"], ... };
  }
  ```
- Similarly remove `_mixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteralDeserializer`, `_mixedTypesCasesModelDeserializer`, `_stringAndArrayCasesStringDeserializer` helpers and update model deserializers to inline.

## Key Files

- `src/components/type-expression.tsx` — `getTypeExpression()`, `case "union"` (~line 184)
- `src/components/model-files.tsx` — `namedUnions` filter (~line 111), `outputUnions` filter (~line 201), `inputUnions` filter (~line 194)
- `src/utils/serialization-predicates.ts` — serializer/deserializer predicates
- `src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx` — `needsTransformation()` and serializer logic
- `test/e2e/http/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` — ensure `experimental-extensible-enums: true`

## Reference Files

- **Legacy output** (correct): `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/src/index.d.ts`
  - `EnumsOnlyCases.lr: "left" | "right" | "up" | "down"` ✅
  - `MixedLiteralsCases.stringLiteral: "a" | 2 | 3.3 | true` ✅
  - `MixedTypesCases.model: Cat | "a" | number | boolean` ✅
  - `StringAndArrayCases.string: string | string[]` ✅
  - No `LR`, `UD`, `EnumsOnlyCasesLr`, `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral`, etc. type aliases ✅
- **Legacy tspconfig**: `submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/test/modularIntegration/generated/type/union/tspconfig.yaml` (has `experimental-extensible-enums: true`)
- **New emitter output** (wrong): `test/e2e/generated/type/union/src/models/models.ts`

## Validation

1. `pnpm test` — all unit/scenario tests must pass
2. `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` — smoke test must pass
3. Verify `EnumsOnlyCases.lr` is `"left" | "right" | "up" | "down"` (not `EnumsOnlyCasesLr`)
4. Verify `MixedLiteralsCases.stringLiteral` is `"a" | 2 | 3.3 | true` (not a named alias)
5. Verify `MixedTypesCases.model` is `Cat | "a" | number | boolean` (not a named alias)
6. Verify `StringAndArrayCases.string` is `string | string[]` (not a named alias)
7. Verify no `LR`, `UD`, `EnumsOnlyCasesLr`, `_MixedLiteralsCasesStringLiteral`, `_MixedTypesCasesModel`, `_StringAndArrayCasesString` type aliases exist
8. Verify no `enumsOnlyCasesLrSerializer`, `enumsOnlyCasesUdSerializer` helper functions exist
9. Verify no `<Unresolved Symbol>` strings in any generated file
10. Verify e2e tests for `type/union` pass

phases_metadata.name: Flavor Parity — Match Legacy Emitter Flavor Behavior
phases_metadata.description: Ensure the new emitter correctly handles the 'flavor' option ('core' vs 'azure') with full parity to the legacy emitter. Core flavor should use @typespec/ts-http-runtime and NOT generate Azure-specific features (LRO, paging, logger, DefaultAzureCredential). Azure flavor should use Azure SDK packages. No 'branded' option support. Flavor must be set explicitly or not at all (defaulting to 'core').

phases_metadata.name: Spector Integration Tests — End-to-End Validation
phases_metadata.description: Set up infrastructure to generate client libraries from Spector specs, run them against the Spector mock server, and validate end-to-end behavior. Uses Approach C (Hybrid): separate generation script + Vitest globalSetup for server lifecycle. This replaces the legacy emitter's modularIntegration test suite.

scenario_audit_metadata.description: Comprehensive audit of semantic differences between NEW (http-client-js) and LEGACY (autorest.typescript) scenario test expectations. Conducted by comparing all 91 matched .md files across both test suites. The LEGACY emitter output is the source of truth - all regression tasks specify that the expected state matches the legacy test.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: Runtime Package Migration (@azure-rest/core-client → @typespec/ts-http-runtime)
intentional_changes.description: The new emitter targets @typespec/ts-http-runtime instead of @azure-rest/core-client. This is the intended design direction. No action needed unless Azure flavor detection requires @azure-rest/core-client.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: Type-Only Import Modifiers (import type)
intentional_changes.description: The new emitter adds the type keyword to type-only imports per TypeScript best practices. This is correct behavior.

intentional_changes.title: INTENTIONAL: expandUrlTemplate Import from Runtime
intentional_changes.description: expandUrlTemplate utility is now imported from the runtime package instead of a static-helpers path. This is part of the runtime consolidation.

risks.title: Output Parity Failures
risks.description: Generated code may differ from legacy emitter in ways that break existing Azure SDK consumers.
risks.mitigation: Run scenario tests incrementally during each phase. Accept only cosmetic differences (import ordering, whitespace).

risks.title: Alloy Version Incompatibilities
risks.description: Alloy API changes between versions may require migration work.
risks.mitigation: Pin to @alloy-js/*@0.22.0. Update deliberately with version bumps.

risks.title: TCGC API Surface Changes
risks.description: TCGC types may evolve during development, requiring adaptation.
risks.mitigation: Pin TCGC version. Monitor TCGC releases and adapt incrementally.

risks.title: Self-Import Bugs
risks.description: Separate SourceFile elements for related declarations (types + serializers) cause Alloy to generate self-imports.
risks.mitigation: Always unify declarations and serializers in same SourceFile via ModelFiles pattern.

risks.title: Static Helper Loading Complexity
risks.description: 21 helper files with varied formats need proper refkey registration and source loading.
risks.mitigation: Start with refkey declarations. Load helper sources as-is. Test each helper independently.

risks.title: Performance Regression
risks.description: Alloy framework overhead may slow code generation for very large service specs.
risks.mitigation: Benchmark against legacy emitter on large specs. Alloy is optimized for codegen.
